{{Quote|My name is Wald. My boss, Watto, he asked me to do a number of things in addition to my regular daily chores.|Wald, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:WW">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Wald's Woes|Tatooine}}</ref>}}
'''W. Wald''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] who owned [[Wald's Parts]] after [[Watto/Legends|Watto]] passed the business to him on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and was a childhood friend of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] when both were [[Youngling/Legends|young]] [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] workers. Wald spoke [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]].

==Biography==
===Childhood===
[[File:WattoUrchins.jpg|thumb|Wald being chased by Watto|left]]
Born in [[38 BBY/Legends|38 BBY]],<ref name="DB" /> Wald was a friend of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] in their childhood years. He lived on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] with his family, which had moved to Mos Espa only shortly before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], and helped Anakin as he built a [[Podracer/Legends|podracer]].<ref name=":0">{{Film|I}}</ref> Wald was known as a petty [[Thief/Legends|thief]] and vandal, and [[Graffiti/Legends|graffiti]] proclaiming [[Watto/Legends|Watto]] to be a "dirty old bird" was attributed to him. Wald always shared his illicit earnings with his friends, and his friendship with Anakin Skywalker was strong.<ref name="DB" />

===Adult life===
{{Quote|I'm afraid you will have to talk to Watto if you need something. I have too much to do... yes, far too much to do.|Wald, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:WW" />}}
In the years following Anakin's departure, Wald remained close friends with [[Kitster Chanchani Banai|Kitster Banai]] and Anakin's mother, [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|Shmi]]. Shmi gave Wald Anakin’s tools and he took a job working in [[Wald's Parts|Watto's salvage yard]], but never matched Anakin's skills in repairing old hardware. Shmi also gave Wald a [[Swoop/Legends|swoop bike]] design that Anakin left in his room. With Kitster help, Wald manage to build Anakin's bike design and manage win his freedom, but Wald admitted it wasn't fast as a [[Podracer/Legends|podracer]], but still pretty fast. He would later attend the wedding of Shmi and [[Cliegg Lars/Legends|Cliegg Lars]], who freed her from Watto.<ref name="Tatooine Ghost">''[[Tatooine Ghost]]''</ref>

[[File:Wald.png|thumb|left|Overburdened, the adult Wald would often stress out.]]
After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Wald was often overburdened with jobs in addition to the normal chores while working for Watto. For example, when there was a problem with large [[Tatooine mynock]]s slipping into Watto's junkyard at night to eat the power cables, Watto told Wald to solve the problem. Fortunately for Wald, a traveler who was looking for a missing droid part agreed to help Wald and went to the location just outside of Mos Espa where the mynocks roamed, thinning out the population drastically. The traveler agreed to do two more jobs for Wald. A gang called the [[Laser Flits]] had ambushed a delivery of [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] parts for one of Wald and Watto's customers and stolen ten swoop parts. The traveler went to the Laser Flits' headquarters and retrieved the parts, killing at least ten gang members. The other job was a simple delivery job. Some spare parts were to be delivered to a [[Qord D'Baelor]] customer waiting in [[Mos Espa Starport]]. Having completed the jobs, the traveler left. Wald appreciated the benevolence of the traveler, whom he ended up considering a friend.<ref name="SWG:WW" />
[[File:Luke-Leia.jpg|thumb|Wald meet Luke and Leia, the children of his best friend Anakin.]]
When Watto retired, he turned over the salvage shop's rights to Wald. He renamed the shop [[Wald's Parts]] and continued to do a meager business during the early years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Wald had encountered a stranger who asked him of Anakin Skywalker. Unknown to Wald, the person was [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the son of his old friend Anakin Skywalker.<ref name="ANHLLS">''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]''</ref> When Kitster [[Mission to Tatooine (Killik Twilight)|stole]] the ''[[Killik Twilight]]'' [[Moss painting/Legends|moss painting]], Wald helped him escape from [[Jergo]]. Unknown to anyone, Kitster stole Wald's swoop to get to the [[Arch Canyon/Legends|Arch Canyon]] to find a disguised couple, a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] and [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]. With Kitster's wife [[Tamora Spice|Tamora]], Wald then later provided information to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] on where Kitster had fled with the moss painting. Wald told [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] the swoop bike, Kister took was design by his best friend Anakin Skywalker. Leia, however, was quietly infuriated that he refused to believe Anakin Skywalker had grown up to become Darth Vader, unable in his mind to reconcile the joyous young child he had known with the monster of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. Wald was also unaware of his return to Tatooine and the actions he took in attempting to rescue his mother.<ref name="Tatooine Ghost" />

==Skills and abilities==
Wald had little combat experience. When fighting, his special move was a tumble roll at the enemy's feet.<ref name="H2H" />

==Equipment==
Wald had a bag of marbles, which he used as a weapon.<ref name="H2H" />

==Behind the scenes==
Wald was played by [[Warwick Davis]],<ref name=":0" /> who also played [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]] in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']],<ref name="Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi">{{Film|VI}}</ref> as well as [[Weazel/Legends|Weazel]] in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']]''<ref name=":0" />'' and various other characters in the series.

In a [[Content cut from Star Wars#Episode I The Phantom Menace|deleted scene]] Greedo accuses [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] of cheating in the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] [[Podracing/Legends|pod race]] which resulted in Anakin attacking Greedo until being stopped by [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]]. Following the altercation, Wald reminds Greedo not to keep up this kind of behavior or he will meet a bad end<ref>[[Content cut from Star Wars#Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars: Episode I: The Phantom Menace'' deleted scenes]]</ref> which he eventually does [[0 BBY/Legends|32 years later]].<ref name="Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope">{{Film|IV}}</ref> ''[[Star Wars Journal: Anakin Skywalker]]'' erroneously claims that Wald ''himself'' was the Rodian who got in a fight with Anakin, despite the two of them being friends.

Wald appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Star Wars video games#Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:WW" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> He was added as part of the quest titled "[[W:c:swg:Wald's Woes|Wald's Woes]]" with the release of "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 2|Chapter 2: The Talus Incident]]," on [[August 1]], [[2006]].<ref name="SonyForumsCite-Chapter 2: The Talus Incident Publish Notes, August 1st">{{SonyForumsCite|full_url=http://soe.lithium.com/swg/board/message?board.id=DevDiscussion&message.id=58871|text="Chapter 2: The Talus Incident" Publish Notes, August 1st}}</ref>
